pattern: A word qualifies only if every one of its prefixes is also a word, so build words up from length one and keep the longest, breaking ties by smallest lexicographic order.
hints: A valid word must have each of its prefixes present in the dictionary too.
Store the words in a set and check, for each word, that every prefix of length one through its full length exists.
Among valid words pick the longest; if several share that length pick the lexicographically smallest.

edge_inputs.description: straight prefix chain builds the full word
tie broken by lexicographic order over length
no single-letter root so nothing can be built
single one-letter word is trivially buildable
missing intermediate prefix invalidates the long word
two independent chains, longer one wins
two equal-length valid words, smaller one chosen
no buildable word returns empty string

stress.description: random prefix chains plus noise words to exercise the prefix test
